# Flask-BasicAuth

[build status]

Flask-BasicAuth is a Flask extension that provides an easy way to protect
certain views or your whole application with HTTP [basic access
authentication][basic access authentication].

## Links

- [Documentation](http://flask-basicauth.readthedocs.org/)
- [Issue Tracker](http://github.com/jpvanhal/flask-basicauth/issues)
- [Code](http://github.com/jpvanhal/flask-basicauth/)
- [Development Version](http://github.com/jpvanhal/flask-basicauth/zipball/master#egg=Flask-BasicAuth-dev)

## Changelog

Here you can see the full list of changes between each Flask-BasicAuth
release.

### 0.2.0 (June 15, 2013)

- Added Python 3 support.

### 0.1.1 (May 20, 2013)

- Fixed an issue where attempting to authenticate with password containing one
  or more colons was failing with "too many values to unpack" error (Michael
  Wallace).

### 0.1.0 (April 30, 2012)

- Initial public release

[basic access authentication]: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basic_access_authentication
[build status]: http://travis-ci.org/jpvanhal/flask-basicauth

modules.name: flask_basicauth
documentation:
flask.ext.basicauth
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Flask-BasicAuth is a Flask extension that provides an easy way to protect
certain views or your whole application with HTTP basic access
authentication.

:copyright: (c) 2013 Janne Vanhala.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

classes.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth

methods.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth.init_app
documentation:
Initialize this BasicAuth extension for the given application.

```
param app

a  instance
```

methods.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth.check_credentials
documentation:
Check if the given username and password are correct.

By default compares the given username and password to
`HTTP_BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME` and `HTTP_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD`
configuration variables.

```
param username

a username provided by the client

param password

a password provided by the client

returns

 if the username and password combination was correct,
and  otherwise.
```

methods.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth.authenticate
documentation:
Check the request for HTTP basic access authentication header and try
to authenticate the user.

:returns: `True` if the user is authorized, or `False` otherwise.

methods.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth.challenge
documentation:
Challenge the client for username and password.

This method is called when the client did not provide username and
password in the request, or the username and password combination was
wrong.

```
returns

a  with 401 response code, including
the required authentication scheme and authentication realm.
```

methods.name: flask_basicauth.BasicAuth.required
documentation:
A decorator that can be used to protect specific views with HTTP
basic access authentication.

A Flask extension for adding HTTP basic access authentication to the
application.

```
param app

a  instance. Defaults to . If no
application is provided on creation, then it can be provided later on
via .
```
